sections_cited: section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI ITRA 187 of 2021 ____________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE ____________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on office objection Nos.01 & 27 2. For orders on CMA No.727/2026 3. For hearing of main case 04.05.2026 Mr. Muhammad Bilal Bhatti, advocate for the applicant This is time-barred reference application pending since 2021 and office objections were required to be complied with. Further diary demonstrates that the applicant has not made any effort to have the matter listed and / or heard. On 14.01.2025, order was passed granting one week’s time to comply with the office objections, however, order has not been complied with. Be that as it may, the reference application is demonstrated to be time-barred. Learned counsel does not dispute the office objection, however, has not filed any application under Section 5 of the Limitation Act, 1908, however, counsel has made no effort to articulate any reason for the delay to be condonded. In view hereof, this reference application found to be time-barred, hence, the same is dismissed as being time- barred. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 133 of the Income Tax Ordinance, 2001. Judge Judge Amjad
